mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline when FEDFUNDS declines over consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent within a single month, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in residential housing starts signals a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate profit margins, yielding negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST does not predict negative 60-day forward returns more often than chance (Rank IC > -0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of cooling consumer credit on equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS and RSAFS both decline during a tightening phase.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding corporate revenue potential, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations across all market conditions over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with consecutive quarters of positive GDPC1 growth (correlation coefficient < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 198.0, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and driving down forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL remains above 198.0 compared to periods below that threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with rising consumer credit balances indicates an overheating economy that historically precedes Federal Reserve policy pivots, generating negative equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns in more than 45% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, a sustained drop in industrial production signals capacity utilization collapse, which disproportionately harms cyclical equities and depresses forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns do not decline when INDPRO falls while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% (t-test p > 0.05).
